speaker: T
text: One half plus one half equals two halves or one whole

speaker: T
text: In a fraction the numbers have names the top number is the numerator and the bottom number is the denominator

speaker: T
text: The denominator tells us how many pieces make up one whole the numerator tells us how many of those pieces were dealing with

speaker: T
text: The two in this case is a common denominator shared by both fractions

speaker: T
text: When you add fractions with the same denominator you add the numerators while the denominator stays the same

speaker: T
text: Each of these pieces is one divided by four or one fourth of the whole we can see that one fourth plus one fourth equals two fourths or one half

speaker: T
text: Lets say we want to add one fourth of this pie to one half of another one

speaker: T
text: Cutting pie one fourth and one half are fractions with different denominators

speaker: T
text: To add them we need to find their LCD or lowest common denominator

speaker: T
text: Its basically the lowest common multiple of the fractions denominators

speaker: T
text: For our denominators two and four that number is four we want to write all of our fractions as fractions of four

speaker: T
text: One fourth already is and as we saw before one half and two fourths are really the same thing

speaker: T
text: Now that we have a common denominator we can just add the numerators two fourths plus one fourth equals three fourths

speaker: T
text: Subtraction is the same idea

speaker: T
text: As long as we have a common denominator its just a matter of subtracting the numerators

speaker: T
text: If we dont have a common denominator we find the lowest common denominator and rewrite our fractions to match
